target.title: fix(media-understanding): resolve image model input from catalog, preserve explicit text-only (#92104)
target.body:
## Summary

Fix the image/pdf tool failing with "Model does not support images" for models whose `input` field is omitted in `models.providers` or agent `models.json`. Preserve explicit `input` overrides so existing text-only configurations are not silently changed.

## Root Cause

In `resolveImageRuntime`, `resolveProviderModelInput` defaults to `["text"]` when the user omits `input` from their model config. The resolution chain prefers inline matches over the bundled catalog, so the resolved model lacks image capability — even though the catalog declares it as `["text","image"]`.

The fix adds a catalog fallback gated by `hasExplicitModelInput`, which checks both `cfg.models.providers` and the agent-local `models.json` (real shape: `{ providers: { "<id>": { models: [...] } } }`). When no explicit input is declared, the bundled catalog's image capability is merged. When input IS explicit (any value, including `["text"]`), the user's choice is preserved unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Models with omitted `input` in models.providers or agent models.json → catalog fills `["text","image"]` → image tool proceeds. Models with explicit `input` → user choice preserved → tool rejects correctly.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x64 (RHEL 8), Node.js v24.13.1, pnpm v11.2.2, openclaw at 79d7defd0b

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node /tmp/repro-92104.mjs
pnpm test -- --run src/media-understanding/image.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix** — reproduction script output (real terminal):
```
  PASS  Omitted input (no cfg, no models.json)
        before: input=["text"] → "Model does not support images" ❌
        after:  input=["text","image"] catalogUsed=true → proceeds

  PASS  Explicit input: ['text'] in cfg.models.providers
        before: input=["text"] → "Model does not support images" ❌
        after:  input=["text"] catalogUsed=false → rejects

  PASS  Explicit input: ['text'] in agent models.json (providers shape)
        before: input=["text"] → "Model does not support images" ❌
        after:  input=["text"] catalogUsed=false → rejects

  PASS  Omitted input (model entry exists but no input field)
        before: input=["text"] → "Model does not support images" ❌
        after:  input=["text","image"] catalogUsed=true → proceeds

  Result: 4/4 passed
```

Test suite output:
```
 Tests  28 passed (28)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Duration  10.60s
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- `input` omitted entirely → catalog supplies `["text","image"]` → proceeds ✅
- `input: ["text"]` in `cfg.models.providers` → catalog skipped → rejects correctly ✅
- `input: ["text"]` in agent `models.json` (`providers` shape) → catalog skipped → rejects correctly ✅
- Model entry exists but `input` field absent → catalog fills → proceeds ✅
- Zero regressions (28/28 tests pass)

**What was not tested**: End-to-end gateway with live API keys (requires real credentials and running gateway).

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` — 28/28
- [x] Reproduction script: 4/4 scenarios (omitted input, cfg explicit, models.json explicit, no-input-field)
- [x] `models.providers` explicit input preserved — test "respects explicit input: ['text'] in models.providers"
- [x] Agent `models.json` explicit input preserved — test "respects explicit input: ['text'] in agent models.json"

---
*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
target.author: zhangqueping
